moduleTitle: Identification, assessment, and management of dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities
questionText: Does your organization consider environmental information about your clients/investees as part of your due diligence and/or environmental dependencies, impacts, risks and/or opportunities assessment process?
questionKo: 귀사는 consider environmental information about your clients/investees as part of your due diligence 및/또는 환경 의존성, impacts, 리스크 및/또는 기회 assessment 프로세스입니까?
simpleHelp: Considering environmental information about clients/investees in the initial phases of a financial institution's assessment of dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities and/or as part of their due diligence process helps data users and the organization understand their value chain's exposure to environmental risks and opportunities. This question helps attest to the robustness of an organization's assessment of environmental issues to data users.

options: No, but we plan to within the next two years
No, and we do not plan to within the next two years
optionsKo: No, but we plan to within the next two years
No, 및 we do not plan to within the next two years

columns.text: Explain why you do not consider environmental information
columns.textKo: 그 이유를 설명 you do not consider environmental information

guidance.text:
This question appears for organizations in the Financial Services sector who report in 1.10 that they undertake banking, investing and "General(non-life)" insurance underwriting activities. The question does not appear for organizations who report that they only insure "Life and/or Health".
Rows are presented according to the reported organizational activities.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 appears for organizations in the 금융 서비스 sector who report in 1.10 that they undertake banking, investing 및 "일반(non-life)" insurance underwriting activities. The 문항 does not appear for organizations who report that they only insure "Life 및/또는 Health". 행 are presented according to the 보고 organizational activities.

guidance.text:
General 
For each of your financial portfolios, disclose whether you consider environmental information about your clients/investee companies as part of your client screening, dependencies, impact, risk and/or opportunities assessment, and/or due diligence process. Incorporating environmental information into business processes can take many forms. This assessment is dependent on the organization's client base and scale of business.
Environmental information about your clients/investees refers to how your clients/investees perform against different environmental indicators, and how they identify, assess, and manage their environmental dependencies, impacts, risks, and/or opportunities.
For the purpose of this question focus on your commercial/corporate clients.
Some examples include:
Incorporating clients' environmental information in borrower and deal-level credit risk and other pre-lending assessments such as due diligence and "know your client" processes.
Incorporating investees' environmental information in fund allocation and investment dependencies, impact, risk and/or opportunities assessment processes.
Incorporating policyholders' environmental information in insurance underwriting due diligence processes.
We consider environmental information (column 1) 
Select "Yes" if you have any process in place for considering environmental information about clients/investees as part of your due diligence and/or environmental dependencies, impacts, risks and/or opportunities assessment process, regardless of how thorough it is. You will be able to provide further details in the subsequent questions.
Explain why you do not consider environmental information and your plans to address this in the future (column 2) 
This column only appears if any "No…" option is selected in column 1 "We consider environmental information".
If you do not consider environmental information, explain your reasons and include any plans to address this in the future.
guidance.textKo: 일반 For each of your financial 포트폴리오s, disclose whether you consider environmental information about your clients/investee companies 의 일부로 your client screening, dependencies, impact, risk 및/또는 기회 assessment, 및/또는 실사 프로세스. Incorporating environmental information into business 프로세스es can take many forms. This assessment is dependent on the 조직의 client base 및 scale of business. Environmental information about your 고객/투자대상 refers to how your 고객/투자대상 perform against different environmental indicators, 및 how they identify, assess, 및 manage their 환경 의존성, impacts, 리스크, 및/또는 기회. 에 대한 purpose of this 문항 focus on your commercial/corporate clients. Some examples include: Incorporating clients' environmental information in bor행er 및 deal-level credit risk 및 other pre-lending assessments such as 실사 및 "know your client" 프로세스es. Incorporating investees' environmental information in fund allocation 및 investment dependencies, impact, risk 및/또는 기회 assessment 프로세스es. Incorporating 정책holders' environmental information in insurance underwriting 실사 프로세스es. We consider environmental information (열 1) Select "예" 만약 you have 하나 이상 프로세스 in place for considering environmental information about 고객/투자대상 의 일부로 your 실사 및/또는 환경 의존성, impacts, 리스크 및/또는 기회 assessment 프로세스, regardless of how thorough it is. You will be able to provide further details in the subsequent 문항. 그 이유를 설명 you do not consider environmental information 및 your plans to address this in the future (열 2) This 열 only appears 만약 하나 이상 "No…" option is 선택 in 열 1 "We consider environmental information". 만약 you do not consider environmental information, explain your reasons 및 include 하나 이상 plans to address this in the future.

guidance.text: Financial institutions consider environmental information about clients/investees as part of the due diligence and/or risk assessment process for all relevant portfolios.
guidance.textKo: Financial institutions consider environmental information about 고객/투자대상 의 일부로 the 실사 및/또는 risk assessment 프로세스 for all 관련 포트폴리오s.

guidance.text:
Due diligence: research or investigation performed by the financial services company before entering into an agreement or a financial transaction with another party.
Portfolio: in the context of this questionnaire your portfolio is the entire collection of your core financing activities and insurance policies that you offer. For banking, this is the entire collection of products, securities and loans held on your balance sheet for which you own the receivable stream. For asset managers, this is the entire collection of your products and investments that you hold and/or manage on behalf of your clients. For asset owners, this is the entire collection of products, funds and investments owned and controlled by your company. For investment portfolios, asset managers should consider discretionary investments, those where the company has discretion over investment decisions. For insurance underwriting, this is the entire collection of products and insurance policies you provide to your clients.
guidance.textKo: 실사: research 또는 investigation performed by the 금융 서비스 company before entering into an agreement 또는 a financial transaction with another party. 포트폴리오: in the context of this 질문서 your 포트폴리오 is the entire collection of your core financing activities 및 insurance policies that you offer. For banking, this is the entire collection of products, securities 및 loans held on your balance sheet for which you own the receivable stream. For 자산운용사, this is the entire collection of your products 및 investments that you hold 및/또는 manage on behalf of your clients. For asset owners, this is the entire collection of products, funds 및 investments owned 및 controlled by your company. For investment 포트폴리오s, 자산운용사 해야 합니다 consider discretionary investments, those 여기서 the company has discretion over investment decisions. For insurance underwriting, this is the entire collection of products 및 insurance policies you provide to your clients.

criteria.criteria:
ROUTE A)
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed.
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this route.
OR
NON-DISCLOSURE ROUTE)
Column 'We consider environmental information' NOT completed in all rows - 0/8 points
A maximum of 0/8 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 경로 A) 표시된 칸(셀)의 개수에 비례하여 완성된 칸(셀)마다 점수가 부여됩니다. 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다. 또는 비공개 경로) 열 'We consider environmental information' NOT 완성 in all 행 - 0/8 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 0/8점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed as follows:
ROUTE A)
'Yes' selected in column 'We consider environmental information' - 1/1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
'No, but we plan to within the next two years' selected in column 'We consider environmental information' - 0.5/1 points
A maximum of 0.5/1 points is available for this route.
OR
NON-DISCLOSURE ROUTE)
Column 'We consider environmental information' NOT completed in all rows - 0/2 points
A maximum of 0/2 points is available for this route.

Column 'We consider environmental information' completed in any row - 0/0 points
A maximum of 0/0 points is available for this route.
OR
NON-DISCLOSURE ROUTE)

moduleTitleKo: 환경 의존성, 임팩트, 리스크 및 기회의 식별, 평가 및 관리
simpleHelpKo: Considering environmental information about 고객/투자대상 in the initial phases of a financial institution's assessment of dependencies, impacts, 리스크, 및 기회 및/또는 의 일부로 their 실사 프로세스 helps 데이터 사용자 및 the organization understand their 가치사슬's exposure to environmental 리스크 및 기회. This 문항 helps attest to the robustness of an 조직의 assessment of 환경 이슈 to 데이터 사용자.
